column1: # metric budget_ms baseline_ms
# baseline_ms captured on the m8-benchmark CI job's own ubuntu-latest runner.
# keel_check and keel_build_cold: workflow run 28676356124 (PR #24,
# binary-search name lookups + the #18 GOCACHE pre-warm).
# keel_build_incremental: the `keel build` up-to-date cutoff (stamp beside the
# output binary; see compiler/keelc-driver/src/build_cache.rs) makes the
# unchanged rebuild a verified no-op, so all three KDR-0019 budgets are
# enforced in CI: --enforce fails a PR that exceeds budget_ms or regresses
# >5% over baseline_ms. Measured 16 ms on this runner (workflow run
# 28715590592, PR #43); baseline_ms is deliberately pinned at 100, not 16,
# because the +5% rule was sized for hundreds-of-ms metrics and would fail on
# scheduler jitter at no-op scale. >105 ms means the cutoff stopped being a
# no-op; the 1000 ms budget still catches a fully broken cutoff (full
# pipeline rerun measured ~1121 ms on this runner).
# keel_build_cold's 9451 ms sits within 6% of its 10000 ms budget; if the
# runner fleet's variance trips the gate without a compiler change, that is
# the signal to right-size the corpus or budget via KDR, not to widen silently.
keel_check 300 228
keel_build_cold 10000 9451
keel_build_incremental 1000 100
